ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Amɛ dɩ nal lɛ tʋʋsɩ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ, Wɩɩsɩ bɩ sɩ maakyiye kpa kyɛ ʋ buloŋ. Sʋʋkpʋŋ wɩbɔŋ aa bɩ kaŋ marɩ layɩ rɛ ʋ tɩɩna yaa gɛɛ.”
translated: They said that the reason why God created the universe is that God wanted to create a place to live. Darkness was there and God said let there be light in the world.

ref: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
text: Lagɩlagɩ no, ŋ sɩ basɩ tɩya ma anɩɩ Ilaagya paalɩ ko rɛ. Ba kana ʋ yaa ʋ gɛɛ hʋ ba aa kyɛ anɩɩ ba aa saba gɛɛ Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ tɩyaŋ.” Yesu tɩɩbɩ biye gyɩŋbɔŋ aa kana Matiwu
translated: Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me—one who is eating with me. They were saddened, and one by one they said to him, “Surely you don’t mean me?” Jesus said to them, “It is one of the Twelve.”

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Ʋ aa taa nala hʋ ba mʋ teŋ, ʋ sii mʋ gyɩŋ dogimo kɩdɩgɩ nyuu dɩ ʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ.
translated: They said that when he was going to the forest, he met a certain hunter who was carrying a dead monkey on his shoulder.

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ kpa namagɩsɩ a basɩ wɩya yʋga tɩya ba. Ʋ gyɩ maga namaga tɩya ba rɛ a baa, “Baal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ siye a kaŋ ʋ kɩdoho mʋ dɩ ʋ gyaasɩ.
translated: They went and met some people who were selling things. One old woman selling things said to them, "Come and buy things here so that I can buy something to eat."

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Ma kaŋ ko tɩya ŋ.”
translated: Then Jesus said, "Let it be so now."

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu wɩya taalɩ Siiriya paalʋʋ buloŋ. Nala sii kaŋ ba wɩɩla tɩmma kɩ ko ʋ lee. Gbarɩga abee kpuŋkpuŋ-telle abee nala gyɩŋbɔmɔ aa kana abee wɩɩla iriŋ iriŋ buloŋ tɩmma rɛ ba kaŋ ko, Yesu tɩɩbɩ ba buloŋ.
translated: Jesus went to the region of Syria and stayed there. Many people heard about him and they came to follow him. They brought people who were paralyzed, those who were troubled by unclean spirits, and those who were sick with various diseases, and Jesus healed them.

ref: Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs.
text: Nala hʋ fa aa we doŋ a naa wɩɩ hʋ aa yaa baal hʋ abee porikusi hʋ, ba basɩ wɩɩ hʋ tɩya nala hʋ buloŋ.
translated: The child is crying because he wants to go to his mother, but his mother has gone to the farm, so he is crying loudly.

ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ɩ kɛ yaa gɛɛ ŋ aa baa ɩ yaa. Nyɛ rɛ sɩ leŋ dɩ á yaa wɩya hʋ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa kyɛɛ.” Ɛɛ rɛ Gyɔɔŋ laa nyʋwa, a fo Yesu wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ.
translated: He said to Jesus, "I will follow you wherever you go." Jesus said to him, "The foxes have holes and the birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head." He said to another, "Follow me," but he said, "Lord, first let me go and bury my father."

ref: The worms there never die, and the fire never stops burning.
text: ‘Lee hʋ kyaaŋkyʋwana aa sɩ di ɩ teŋbii deŋdeŋ a bɩ sɩ sʋba, ka diŋ hʋ mɛ bɩ sɩ dɩsɩ.’
translated: The farmer's children are not allowed to walk on the newly planted farm, so that they will not destroy it.

ref: At last the woman died.
text: Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ haaŋ hʋ mɛ sii sʋba.
translated: The woman is going to the farm with her child.

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Ba saba rɛ Wɩɩsɩ teŋ tɩyaŋ a baa, “Ma aa fá nala sikii, ʋ rɛ kpɩya ma aa lɩɩ wɩɩsʋllɩ kɩna a kpʋ tɩya ŋ.” Dɩ mamaa rɛ kɔnɩ fa gyɩŋ wɩɩ no memii, ma fa bɩ sɩ kpa kyogisi kɩ tɩya nala hʋ aa bɩ kaŋ wɩkyogii.
translated: The rabbit said to the elephant, "I will not be able to run, but I will be able to enter the elephant's house and hide." The elephant's mother said to her child, "Do not worry, I will go and dig a hole for you to hide in, and you will be safe."

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Wɩya hʋ ma aa sɩ basɩ, ba bɩ sɩ yaa ma tɩɩ wɩbasɩya. Ma Kuwo Wɩɩsɩ rɛ sɩ leŋ dɩ ʋ Wiyesi Welii hʋ tɩŋ ma tɩyaŋ a basɩ wɩya no tɩya ma.
translated: The child is crying because he wants a bicycle, but his father doesn't have money to buy one. The elders of Kuwo Wiesi village are going to Wiyesi Welii's house to discuss the matter of the child's bicycle.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Nyɛ rɛ porikudaala hʋ fá mʋ gyʋʋ bee hʋ tɩyaŋ a basɩ wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ aa yaa doŋ abee wɩɩ hʋ aa yaa baala hʋ gyɩ aa kaŋ gyɩŋbɔmɔ hʋ mɛ tɩyaŋ a tɩya bee hʋ nala.
translated: The child is crying because his mother has gone to the farm and he wants to go with her but his father is refusing, saying he should stay and play with his siblings.

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Piita ha fa aa basɩ wɩya hʋ gɛɛ dɩ taal tʋlɩɩ kɩdɩgɩ ko tuu tɔ ba, ka taal lɩɩ taal tʋlɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ a baa, “Ŋ Biikyoolii rɛ nyɛ. Ŋ teŋ fɩyɛla rɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ. Ma gyegili kɩ nɩɩ ʋ nyʋwa.”
translated: The rabbit said to his sister, "Let us go to our mother. Our yam farm is ripe. I will dig and you will gather."

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Wɩɩsɩ aa ko maga nyutʋtʋʋ, lee hʋ buloŋ bil gɛɛ timm a mʋ pele dɩdaana, ka lee bɩl na kyaanɩ.
translated: The rabbit and the monkey went to the forest to look for food to eat, and they found a big tree with ripe fruit.

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Beewɩya Gyɔɔŋ Wɩɩsɩnɩɩfool hʋ ko daga ma wɩtɩɩ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ rɛ ma aa sɩ tɩŋa, aŋka ma bɩ laa ʋ wɩya di, amɛ lampolaala abee gyaŋtɩrɩsɩ kɛ laa ʋ wɩya di rɛ. Here is my attempt at translating the text:

"[The kingdom of heaven is like a master who gave a task to his servants and went on a journey, and he gave them talents, five to one, two to another, and one to another, according to their abilities. The one who received five talents went and traded with them and gained five more. The one who received two talents also gained two more. But the one who received one talent went and dug a hole and hid his master's money. The master of those servants came and settled accounts with them." Jesus told his disciples this parable.

ref: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
text: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ŋ tɩya buloŋ nɛ kyogo ŋ bembii kɩ kyɛ dɩ ʋ bori. Ma hɔŋ daha kɩ gyegili ŋ?”
translated: The rabbit said to his sister, "My sister, what is the reason you are always running away from me? Am I a monster?"

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: amɛ ba weliye rɛ kɩŋkaŋ. Kuwori Solomɔn gyɩ kaŋ kɩna rɛ weliŋ. Amɛ abee gɛɛ buloŋ ʋ gannɩ fa bɩ weliye a maga tɩɩfɩɩla no kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ.
translated: They said the elephant is big. King Solomon's wisdom is bigger than the elephant. They asked for a rope to tie the elephant and a big stick to hit the elephant on the back.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Ɛɛwɩya, ma ta leŋ dɩ kyiye wɩya kyɩl ma. Kyɛɛ buloŋ kaŋ ʋ wɩgyʋʋlɩya rɛ. Ma ta bɩl kpaa wɩdʋma buloŋ pɛ kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ bee ʋ wɩgyʋʋlɩya tɩyaŋ.”
translated: They said, I should take the book and read it. But the book was written in a language I didn't understand. I had to look for someone who could interpret the book for me so that I could understand what was written in it.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Amɛ ba aa kile nala hʋ buloŋ ba lɩɩ gɛɛ, Yesu mʋ gyʋʋ haŋtolibiye hʋ dɩya, a mʋ kaŋ ʋ noŋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ sii.
translated: My father and his family were all in the house, Jesus came quietly to the door, my father got up to open it, he did.

ref: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
text: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ dɩ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ mɛ pele nɩɩ hʋ tʋtʋʋ. Nɩɩpuwo kaŋ boro hʋ kɩ viisi, beewɩya ba fa kyaasɩ puwo hʋ rɛ.
translated: The woman and the man are in the house, but the man is not there. The man's shoes are there, but he himself is not there, he has gone to the farm.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: Dɩ nal buloŋ nɛ we ʋ baga tɩyaŋ, ʋ ta bɩl baa ʋ mɩɩgɩ mʋ dɩya a paa ʋ gannɩ.
translated: The man is going to the farm, he will cut the grass and give it to his wife to cook for dinner.

ref: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
text: Ʋ baa, ‘Mɩyaŋ nɛ yaa Wɩɩsɩ hʋ Abɩraham abee Azɩkɩ abee Gyeekɔbɩ buloŋ gyɩ aa tɩŋa. Wɩɩ no ʋ aa basa, ʋ daga anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ yaa niweye Wɩɩsɩ rɛ, ʋ bɩ yaa sʋʋnɩ Wɩɩsɩ.”’
translated: He said, 'I swear by God, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, that I will not take a thread or a sandal strap or anything that belongs to you, so that you will not say, I made Abraham rich.'

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: Dɩdaana aa ko pele, ɛɛ rɛ baga hʋ tɩɩna yɩrɩ nal hʋ aa deŋ ʋ baga hʋ a basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ yɩrɩ nala hʋ aa ko tʋŋ tʋma baga hʋ tɩyaŋ a tuŋ ba. Ʋ baa dɩ ʋ laasɩya tuŋ nala hʋ aa laa hal ko a mʋ tuu pele nala hʋ a laa sɩya ko tɩyaŋ.
translated: The stranger came to the village, and he was given a place to sleep, but he refused and said he would sleep outside, so he went outside and slept under a tree. In the morning, he was given breakfast, but he refused and said he would eat outside, so he went outside and ate under the tree.

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ wɩgyʋnna hʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Aayɩ, nʋʋ hʋ bɩ sɩ pele á beel-ma buloŋ. Ma mʋ kyɛ yɔbɔ ma tɩɩ nʋʋ.”
translated: They said to the chief of the village, "Chief, we have come to ask for a place to settle. We want to stay here and live with you."
